name: Agile Sprint Delivery — Standard
description: Pre-populated starter content for Agile/Scrum engagements with sprint structure, backlog seed, and velocity-based delivery model.

executiveSummary.content:
This engagement delivers {{customer_name}}'s {{project_name}} initiative using Microsoft's Agile Sprint Delivery methodology. Working in time-boxed sprints, the team will iteratively build, test, and release prioritised features, ensuring continuous value delivery and built-in quality. The engagement is structured around a rolling product backlog that is refined collaboratively with {{customer_name}} stakeholders to ensure alignment with evolving business priorities.

Key business outcomes include accelerated time-to-value through working software every sprint, transparent progress tracking via velocity metrics, and a reduced risk profile thanks to early and frequent feedback loops.

inScope.text: Sprint planning, execution, and retrospective ceremonies for all sprints
Continuous Integration / Continuous Deployment (CI/CD) pipeline setup
User Acceptance Testing (UAT) coordination per sprint
Final solution handover and knowledge transfer session

outOfScope.text: Ongoing managed service or Level 3 support post-handover
Data migration from legacy systems unless explicitly itemised in backlog

agileApproach.deliveryApproach: The engagement will follow a Scrum framework operating in two-week sprints. Each sprint begins with a Sprint Planning session to select backlog items from the prioritised Product Backlog and ends with a Sprint Review (demo to {{customer_name}}) and Retrospective. A daily stand-up (15 min) keeps the team aligned. The Microsoft-nominated Scrum Master facilitates all ceremonies and removes impediments. The {{customer_name}}-nominated Product Owner owns the backlog and attends planning and review sessions.
agileApproach.supportTransitionPlan: In the final sprint, the team will conduct a structured handover: (1) deploy to production environment, (2) deliver runbook and operational documentation, (3) perform a 2-hour knowledge transfer session with {{customer_name}} IT team, (4) remain on call for a 2-week hypercare period post go-live.

sprints.name: Sprint 0 — Foundation
sprints.goals: Environment provisioning, CI/CD pipeline, backlog refinement, team onboarding

sprints.name: Sprint 1 — Core Capability
sprints.goals: Highest-priority backlog items; first working build

sprints.name: Sprint 2 — Feature Build
sprints.goals: Second tier of backlog items; integration tests

sprints.name: Sprint 3 — Hardening
sprints.goals: Bug fixes, performance tuning, UAT support

productBacklog.story: As a developer, I need the Azure DevOps project and repositories configured so that the team can begin feature work
As a developer, I need a CI pipeline that builds and tests on every pull request so that code quality is enforced automatically
As a {{customer_name}} user, I need [primary feature] so that I can achieve [business outcome]
As a {{customer_name}} administrator, I need role-based access control so that data is only accessible to authorised users
As a {{customer_name}} manager, I need a dashboard showing key metrics so that I can monitor business outcomes

deliverables.title: Sprint 0 — Project Kickoff Report
deliverables.description: Documented environment setup, team charter, and refined Product Backlog

deliverables.title: Working Software (per sprint)
deliverables.description: Tested, demo-ready build at the end of each sprint deployed to staging

deliverables.title: UAT Sign-off Documentation
deliverables.description: Completed UAT results signed off by {{customer_name}} Product Owner

deliverables.title: Solution Architecture Document
deliverables.description: As-built architecture diagram and component descriptions

deliverables.title: Runbook & Operations Guide
deliverables.description: Day-2 operations playbook including deployment, rollback, and monitoring procedures

assumptions.text: {{customer_name}} will nominate a Product Owner who is available for planning and review ceremonies (min. 4 hrs/week)
Azure subscription with sufficient quota for the target workload will be provisioned by {{customer_name}} prior to Sprint 0
All third-party dependencies (APIs, data feeds) are accessible and documented before Sprint 1
{{customer_name}} will provide timely UAT feedback within 3 business days of each Sprint Review

customerResponsibilities.text: Assign and maintain an empowered Product Owner for the duration of the engagement
Provision Azure environment, networking, and required licences
Ensure subject matter expert availability for requirements workshops (Sprint 0)
Perform and sign off UAT within agreed timelines

risks.text: Backlog scope creep — mitigated by sprint-level change control and backlog re-prioritisation ceremonies
Product Owner unavailability — mitigated by nominating a deputy and scheduling ceremonies 2 weeks in advance
Third-party API instability — mitigated by stub/mock services during development, with live integration in Sprint 3+

members.responsibilities: Ceremony facilitation, impediment removal, velocity tracking
Solution architecture, hands-on development, code review
Feature development, unit testing, CI/CD maintenance
Test strategy, automated test authoring, UAT coordination

teamStructure.supportTransitionPlan: Hypercare support (best-effort, business hours) for 2 weeks post go-live. Issues triaged by Scrum Master; severity-1 bugs addressed within 4 business hours.
